entries.sourceQuote: We value the experiences that visiting residents/fellows bring to our program, and we want to make sure that house staff from other institutions have every opportunity to come to VUMC and experience what we have to offer. Visiting rotations are subject to approval by the appropriate Program Director.
entries.rationale: lane is future-lane only

entries.sourceQuote: To be eligible for a visiting rotation, you must be in an ACGME-accredited training program and must qualify for licensure or exemption from licensure under the Tennessee Board of Medical Examiners requirements
entries.rationale: lane is future-lane only

entries.sourceQuote: Visiting resident applicants must apply 120 days or more from start date and have met all requirements 30 days or more prior to their requested start date. if this requirement is not met, the visiting resident's request to rotate will be cancelled and they will need to reapply.
entries.rationale: lane is future-lane only

entries.sourceQuote: The Program Coordinator of the VUMC program with which you desire to rotate must initiate a contract /program letter of agreement process at least 100 days before your desired start date .
entries.rationale: lane is future-lane only

entries.sourceQuote: A completed and signed visiting resident application Click here for application . This will need to be approved by the VUMC Program Director. The application requires your NPI number.
entries.rationale: lane is future-lane only

entries.sourceQuote: A reference letter from your Program Director stating whether you are in good standing in an accredited training program.
entries.rationale: lane is future-lane only

entries.sourceQuote: Documentation that your institution will continue to provide your stipend along with benefits, liability and health insurance (can be in the same letter as above).
entries.rationale: lane is future-lane only

entries.sourceQuote: A copy of your malpractice liability insurance with a minimum of $1 million per claimant and $3 million per occurrence, with the exception that Tennessee state employees do not need additional coverage.
entries.rationale: lane is future-lane only

entries.sourceQuote: If you are in a residency/fellowship in Tennessee and do not have your own medical license, a copy of the letter of exemption from licensure (your program or GME Office will have this). Note: If you are not in a Tennessee residency/fellowship and do not have full TN medical license, VUMC will request an exemption from licensure for you.
entries.rationale: lane is future-lane only

entries.sourceQuote: If applicable, a copy of a valid ECFMG certificate and Visa.
entries.rationale: lane is future-lane only

entries.sourceQuote: Immunization Form. This form must be completed by a healthcare provider other than the perspective visiting resident/fellow and sent to the Program Coordinator of the program with which you will rotate.
entries.rationale: lane is future-lane only

entries.sourceQuote: Completion of the consent form from Cisive will initiate the background check. Cisive will contact you with a web link to initiate your background check.
entries.rationale: lane is future-lane only
